_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to collect, process, and compare information and data from primary and secondary sources — including fieldwork and digital tools — with attention to ethical considerations.

_source.w: We are learning to collect, process, and compare information and data from primary and secondary sources — including fieldwork and digital tools — with attention to ethical considerations.
y: Rigorous data collection and processing is the foundation of credible geographical research.
Ethical considerations — informed consent, cultural sensitivity, data privacy — are non-negotiable in professional research.
Comparing data from different sources builds the evidentiary base for robust conclusions.
eg: Designing and conducting fieldwork with attention to ethical protocols — consent, safety, cultural respect.
Collecting and processing secondary data from government databases, satellite imagery, and academic research.
Using digital tools — GIS, statistical software, online databases — to process and analyse data.
Comparing primary data with secondary sources to identify consistencies and discrepancies.
Discussing the ethical considerations of our data collection — especially when researching sensitive communities.
Documenting our data collection and processing methods for transparency and reproducibility.
